name: Business Value (BV)

definition.summary: Business Value measures the magnitude of positive business outcome delivered if this work is completed successfully.
details: Represents incremental impact to the business within a 12–24 month horizon
characteristics: Independent of effort, urgency, and risk
Grounded in measurable or observable outcomes where possible

scoringPrinciples: Score incremental value, not total product value
Use relative comparison across the portfolio
Do not discount for delivery risk (handled elsewhere)
Anything above a score of 5 requires justification

scaleDefinitions.label: Negligible
scaleDefinitions.description: No material customer, revenue, or strategic impact. Would not be noticed if delayed or removed.
examples: Optional polish with no KPI impact

scaleDefinitions.label: Very Low
scaleDefinitions.description: Marginal improvement with very limited reach. No measurable business KPI impact.
examples: Feature for a very small user group

scaleDefinitions.label: Low
scaleDefinitions.description: Small, measurable improvement with limited scope. Impact is real but minor.
examples: Improvement affecting less than 5% of users

scaleDefinitions.label: Moderate-Low
scaleDefinitions.description: Tangible but localized impact. Supports business goals in a limited way.

scaleDefinitions.label: Moderate
scaleDefinitions.description: Clear, measurable business value. Benefits a meaningful segment and supports at least one business objective.
examples: Retention improvement in a defined segment
Cost reduction at team or product level

scaleDefinitions.label: Moderate-High
scaleDefinitions.description: Strong business impact across multiple teams or customer segments. Would be noticed if delayed.
examples: Competitive parity in an important area

scaleDefinitions.label: High
scaleDefinitions.description: Significant contribution to company objectives. Affects a large portion of customers, revenue, or cost structure.
examples: Broad efficiency gains across a function

scaleDefinitions.label: Very High
scaleDefinitions.description: Major driver of business performance. Directly tied to annual goals or strategic initiatives.

scaleDefinitions.label: Critical
scaleDefinitions.description: Among the most valuable initiatives in the portfolio. Failure to deliver would materially harm the business.

scaleDefinitions.label: Existential / Transformational
scaleDefinitions.description: Business survival or transformation level impact. Enables or protects a fundamental business capability with no viable alternative.
examples: Enables a new core business model
Prevents catastrophic revenue or customer loss

governanceGuidance: Require written justification for Business Value scores of 7 or higher
Calibrate scores across products and portfolios
Review score distribution to prevent inflation
Use Business Value scores as an input to decision-making, not the decision itself
